name: override_self_resolve_transient
description: Cool day, HVAC in cool mode at comfort (74F). At 10:00 thermostat briefly reports heat mode (transient glitch). At 10:05 thermostat returns to cool before the 10-minute confirm window expires. Override self-resolves; no override is accepted; classification continues normally.
unsupported_reason: Integration-tier: the override self-resolve path requires the coordinator's _async_thermostat_changed state listener to detect the thermostat returning to the expected mode before the async_call_later confirm timer fires — cancelling the pending override. In the Tier-A harness the confirm timer does not run; the engine's override_self_resolved decision is emitted at the wrong time (10:10 from the timer expiry rather than 10:05 from the thermostat returning), and subsequent classification assertions fail because the event-log cursor is misaligned. Additionally, expect_temp=74.0 assertions are stale post-#249 (engine sets dual-temp 70/74). Deferred to Tier-B HeadlessTarry harness (#236 follow-on) which wires the coordinator state-listener and timer-cancellation path.
notes: Tests the 'transient glitch' vs 'real user change' distinction in the override confirmation window.
Gap 3 (override confirmation delay) is NOT yet in simulate.py — override_detected and override_self_resolved assertions are marked track:integration.

What the simulator CAN test today: after transient thermostat_state_changed (heat) + return to cool, the next classification cycle fires normally (not suppressed), because the simulator does not set manual_override_active from thermostat_state_changed events.
Logic-track assertion at 10:30 verifies classification continues normally (no override suppression).
Once Gap 3 is closed, the integration assertions can be promoted.

events.note: Transient: thermostat briefly reports heat mode. Override detection fires; 10-min confirm window starts.
Thermostat returns to cool mode before 10:05 (transient glitch resolved)
Thermostat returns to cool mode at 10:05 — only 5 minutes elapsed, before confirm window expires. Override self-resolves.
30-min classification cycle. Override should NOT be active — classification should apply normally.

assertions.reason: Initial cool-day classification applied: hvac_mode=cool, target=comfort_cool=74F.
Thermostat changed to heat while classification says cool — pending override detection.
assertions.note: Override detection fires in coordinator _async_thermostat_changed listener.

assertions.reason: Thermostat returned to cool within 5 min (before override_confirm_seconds=600). Override pending window cancelled — no override accepted.
assertions.note: Self-resolve requires coordinator _async_thermostat_changed to cancel the pending confirm timer.

assertions.reason: Override was not confirmed (self-resolved), so classification cycle at 10:30 applies normally — not suppressed.

verdict.summary: Transient thermostat state change self-resolves before confirm window; classification continues normally
verdict.expected_behavior: CA distinguishes transient thermostat glitches from real user overrides using a 10-min confirmation window
